articles.title: Hasbro-Branded Family Entertainment Center Coming to Pigeon Forge in 2027
articles.summary: A Hasbro-themed family entertainment center is set to open in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee in summer 2027, adding a major IP-driven FEC to one of the Southeast's busiest tourist corridors. The project signals continued momentum for branded, IP-led FEC concepts as operators look to differentiate from generic entertainment offerings. For FEC operators and investors, this is a notable example of toy and game IP crossing into location-based entertainment.

articles.title: EVO Entertainment Secures Site for $50M Clearwater, FL Complex
articles.summary: A downtown Clearwater site has been cleared for a $50 million EVO Entertainment complex, representing one of the larger FEC development investments announced this year. EVO's multi-concept model — which typically combines bowling, arcade games, laser tag, and dining — continues to expand aggressively in major markets. The scale of the investment underscores strong operator confidence in the FEC sector's growth trajectory.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Launches Custom Mobile App and Digital Experience Platform
articles.summary: Monster Mini Golf has rolled out a new branded mobile app and connected digital platform featuring gamified score tracking, birthday party reservations, and personalized guest engagement tools across its nationwide locations. The move reflects a growing FEC industry trend toward tech-driven guest experience enhancements that extend engagement beyond the physical visit. Operators looking to modernize their guest touchpoints and drive repeat visits should take note of this model.

articles.title: Comcast Spins Off NBCUniversal — Including Universal Theme Parks — as Independent Company
articles.summary: Comcast has announced plans to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky into a separate publicly traded company, which will include the Universal theme parks segment currently in rapid expansion mode with Epic Universe. The restructuring could affect capital allocation, strategic priorities, and partnership opportunities across the broader attractions and location-based entertainment ecosystem. FEC investors and suppliers with ties to the Universal brand or competing in its markets should monitor how the new entity's leadership shapes its parks strategy.

articles.title: EAG Rebrands as EAG 360 and Adopts 'The Smile Economy' as Industry Tagline
articles.summary: EAG, the UK-based international entertainment and gaming trade event, has rebranded as EAG 360 and introduced 'The Smile Economy' as a unifying theme, alongside a restructured show format with new co-located sector events including CompsocUK, London Gaming Show, and Amuse Expo. The rebrand signals ambitions to broaden the event's reach across the full amusements and attractions spectrum. FEC operators and suppliers active in the UK and European markets should be aware of the expanded event structure when planning their trade show calendars.

articles.title: Semnox Solutions Partners with New Indoor FEC K&K Adventure Zone
articles.summary: Semnox Solutions, a leading provider of cashless payment and venue management technology, has partnered with K&K Adventure Zone, a new indoor entertainment venue. The partnership highlights continued demand for integrated RFID and cashless systems as new FEC openings seek to implement modern operational infrastructure from day one. Operators evaluating venue management platforms should follow Semnox's growing deployment portfolio for real-world performance insights.

articles.title: Fun Spot America Atlanta Closing, Future of ArieForce One Coaster Uncertain
articles.summary: Fun Spot America is shutting down its Atlanta-area theme park, leaving the fate of its signature ArieForce One wooden roller coaster — a highly regarded new addition — in question. The closure is a cautionary tale for FEC and regional attraction operators about the challenges of competing in saturated markets or underperforming real estate situations. Industry observers will be watching whether the coaster asset is relocated or sold, which could signal opportunities for other operators.

articles.title: India's Indoor Entertainment Sector Sees Growing Momentum and Investment
articles.summary: India's indoor entertainment market is gaining traction as rising disposable incomes, urbanization, and a growing middle class fuel demand for FEC-style attractions. The Business Upturn analysis points to accelerating investment in bowling, arcade, and family entertainment formats across major Indian cities. International FEC operators and equipment suppliers looking at emerging markets should consider India's growth potential.

articles.title: Lefty's Alley & Eats Opens Delaware's Largest Indoor Entertainment Destination in Newark
articles.summary: Lefty's Alley & Eats has opened a large-format indoor entertainment complex in Newark, Delaware, expanding beyond its Delaware beach origins to bring bowling, games, and dining to a new suburban market. The venue represents the continued appetite for multi-attraction FEC concepts anchored by food and beverage offerings. Its expansion from a resort market to a year-round suburban location is a model other operators may look to replicate.

articles.title: Kokomo Joe's FEC Adding Multi-Level Go-Kart Track
articles.summary: Kokomo Joe's family entertainment center is investing in a new multi-level go-kart installation, a capital-intensive upgrade that signals the operator's confidence in go-karts as a high-draw, premium attraction. Multi-level tracks have become increasingly popular as FECs seek to maximize entertainment density within limited footprints. This move reflects a broader industry trend toward elevating the go-kart experience to attract older teens and adults.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Releases 29-Country Study on Birthday Party Guest List Trends
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has published research spanning 29 countries examining whether parents limit birthday party guest lists, providing data-driven insights relevant to FEC birthday party program planning and revenue forecasting. Birthday parties remain one of the highest-margin revenue streams for FEC operators, making consumer behavior research in this category directly actionable. The findings could help operators tailor packages, pricing, and capacity planning for party events.

articles.title: Belt Entertainment & Volleys FEC to Close Its Doors
articles.summary: Belt Entertainment & Volleys, a combined entertainment and pickleball concept, has announced it will close, adding to a recent string of FEC closures that highlight the operational risks facing newer hybrid entertainment formats. The closure may reflect challenges in blending trend-driven sports concepts with traditional FEC business models under current cost pressures. FEC operators with similar hybrid models should review their unit economics in light of this and other recent closures.

articles.title: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth Announces Permanent Closure
articles.summary: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth, Texas is permanently closing, continuing a pattern of smaller, independent FEC closures seen across the country. Rising operating costs, competition from larger multi-concept venues, and post-pandemic shifts in consumer spending are frequently cited factors in similar closures. The trend underscores the competitive pressure independent FEC operators face from well-capitalized regional and national chains.

articles.title: Magic Mountain Fun Center East in Columbus to Permanently Close After July 5
articles.summary: Magic Mountain Fun Center East in Columbus, Ohio will permanently close after July 5, marking another independent FEC casualty in a competitive marketplace. The closure follows broader industry pressures on smaller operators including labor costs, lease expenses, and shifting consumer preferences toward larger, more diversified entertainment venues. Operators in similar market positions should evaluate their competitive differentiation and financial runway.

articles.title: Big Air Trampoline Park Opens New Location in Glendale, Arizona
articles.summary: Big Air, one of the largest indoor trampoline park chains in the U.S., is opening a new location in Glendale, Arizona, expanding its footprint in the competitive Phoenix metro market. The opening adds to a strong pipeline of trampoline park openings nationally as the segment continues to attract both independent operators and chain investment. FEC operators in the Southwest should monitor the competitive dynamics as Big Air adds capacity in the region.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Family Entertainment Destination in Greater Noida West, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone has opened a new family entertainment center at PKS Town Central in Greater Noida West, reflecting the accelerating pace of FEC development in India's rapidly urbanizing second-tier cities. The opening is part of a broader expansion by domestic FEC brands looking to capture demand from India's growing middle-class families. International equipment suppliers and concept developers should track India's FEC sector as a significant emerging opportunity.

articles.title: New Adventure Park Unlike Any Other Opens in Atlanta
articles.summary: A new adventure park describing itself as unlike anything else in the country has opened in the Atlanta area, entering a market recently vacated by Fun Spot America. The timing and positioning suggest operators are already moving to capture the customer base left by Fun Spot's closure. FEC developers and investors should watch Atlanta as an active market for attraction openings and consolidation.

articles.title: London-Origin Indoor Entertainment Venue with Virtual Soccer and Interactive Games Opens in Paris
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment concept originating from London — featuring virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food — has opened its first Paris location, demonstrating the cross-border scalability of tech-forward FEC formats. The venue model combining sports simulation with social dining is gaining traction across European urban markets. FEC operators considering experiential gaming and sports entertainment concepts should follow this format's international expansion.

articles.title: High Tide VR Near Bethany Beach Offers Immersive Virtual Reality Entertainment
articles.summary: High Tide VR, a virtual reality entertainment venue near Bethany Beach, Delaware, is positioning itself as a distinctive alternative to traditional beach-area amusements. The venue illustrates how VR attractions are finding viable niches in tourist-dependent markets where novelty and weather-independent entertainment are strong selling points. FEC operators in resort or coastal markets should evaluate VR as both a standalone draw and a complementary attraction.

articles.title: Seder Group and ProFun Launch SPOC, a New Attractions Operating Platform for Saudi Arabia
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia's Seder Group and global attractions advisory firm ProFun have formed SPOC, a new operating company designed to support Saudi Arabia's expanding entertainment, tourism, and cultural attraction sector. The joint venture reflects the massive scale of investment flowing into FEC and attraction operations in the Middle East under Saudi Vision 2030. Suppliers and operators looking to enter the Saudi market should monitor SPOC as a potential channel partner.

articles.title: UK Heatwave Drives Consumers Toward Indoor Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: A UK heatwave is accelerating consumer interest in indoor entertainment as Brits seek climate-controlled alternatives to outdoor activities, benefiting FECs, trampoline parks, and indoor attractions. The trend reinforces the value of indoor FEC formats as weather-resilient businesses with built-in demand spikes during extreme temperature events. UK-based FEC operators should consider how to capture and convert this seasonal surge into longer-term customer relationships.

articles.title: Bingemans' Big Splash Waterpark Adds Typhoon Twister Slide Tower, Expands to 13 Slides
articles.summary: Bingemans in Kitchener, Ontario has unveiled the Typhoon Twister waterslide tower, adding four high-speed slides and bringing its Big Splash waterpark to 13 total slides for summer 2026. The expansion demonstrates how mid-size regional FEC and waterpark operators can drive attendance growth through strategic capital investment in signature attractions. The phased expansion model Bingemans uses offers a practical template for operators planning capacity growth.

articles.title: Arizona's Top 10 Indoor Entertainment Attractions Ranked for 2026
articles.summary: AZ Big Media has published its annual ranking of Arizona's top indoor entertainment attractions, spotlighting the venues competing for consumer attention in one of the Sun Belt's fastest-growing markets. The list provides a useful competitive landscape snapshot for operators and investors evaluating the Arizona market. Rankings like these increasingly influence consumer discovery and can drive meaningful traffic to featured venues.

articles.title: Peppa Pig Theme Park Dallas-Fort Worth Adds Miss Rabbit Meet-and-Greet Experience
articles.summary: Peppa Pig Theme Park Dallas-Fort Worth has introduced a new Miss Rabbit meet-and-greet experience, part of a series of limited-time events designed to drive repeat family visits throughout the season. IP-driven character experiences remain one of the most effective tools for boosting guest engagement and social media shareability at family-focused attractions. FEC operators partnering with or considering IP licensing should take note of how character programming layers value onto existing attractions.

articles.title: Huitt-Zollars Hires LBE Design Veteran Dan Jenkins to Expand Themed Entertainment Practice
articles.summary: Professional services firm Huitt-Zollars has appointed Dan Jenkins, a 20-year themed entertainment veteran, as director of design for its creative development team focused on architecture and experiential design across LBE, hospitality, and retail sectors. The hire signals growing demand for design expertise at the intersection of themed entertainment and location-based experiences. FEC developers and operators planning new builds or major renovations may find expanded options among firms like Huitt-Zollars building out their LBE-focused practices.

articles.title: Adirondack Studios Expands Middle East Presence with New Entities in UAE and Saudi Arabia
articles.summary: Adirondack Studios has established registered entities in Abu Dhabi and Riyadh and promoted senior leadership in Dubai, deepening its footprint in the Middle East's booming themed entertainment and LBE sector. The move positions ADKS to better serve the region's surging pipeline of FEC, cultural, and resort attraction projects. Operators and developers building in the Middle East now have access to expanded local fabrication and creative design capacity from a globally experienced supplier.

articles.title: Disney Imagineering Unveils Hybrid Projection-Animatronic Technology Debuting at Pirates of the Caribbean
articles.summary: Walt Disney Imagineering has revealed a new hybrid technology combining front projection with partially articulated mechanical animatronics, debuting with a pirate figure at Disneyland that transforms from human to skeleton in real time. While developed for theme parks, this category of next-generation animatronic and projection blending has implications for high-end LBE and FEC dark ride developers seeking to elevate attraction storytelling. The technology showcases where the bar for immersive character experiences is being set at the top of the industry.

articles.title: Max-Matching's Owen Zhao on the Rise of IP-Led Location-Based Entertainment in China
articles.summary: Max-Matching founder Owen Zhao outlines how China's LBE market has rapidly matured over 20 years, with the most successful projects now built around strong, long-term IPs with established audience ecosystems. The analysis offers valuable context for operators and investors eyeing the Chinese market or considering how IP strategy shapes attraction viability globally. As IP-led FEC concepts gain traction worldwide, Zhao's framework provides a useful lens for evaluating new development opportunities.
